---
title: "UI/UX"
verify: none # no executable cells
---

What is UI/UX and what how to apply it in practice.

Main content can be found here:
- Figma file where you can explore the practical aspects of app design: [link to Figma](https://www.figma.com/design/kGTVt2mugpQb986YV0NAew/UX%2FUI-Design-lecture-Case-Study?node-id=0-1&t=tnyH4SnsTfqEBkfK-1).
- Slides use for presentation: [link to OneDrive](https://vult-my.sharepoint.com/:b:/g/personal/tomas_smagurauskas_mif_vu_lt/EZWLl--Gq6lOpLP-Qd_JJasBszzkDmX2S7_jZZI4N9YJNA?e=WUAyD6). Will need to login with VU email to access them.

Rest of the notebook to be considered as "quick references", rather than the actual material at the moment.

### What is UI and what is UX

User Interface (UI) focuses on how a product/web looks and interacts visually.

User Experience (UX) focuses on how a product feels and functions.

### UX vs UI

Purpose: UX solves user needs; UI creates visual appeal.

Focus: UX is functionality centered; UI is appearance centered.

Order: UX often comes first (foundation), and UI builds on top (decoration).

## UX

UX design is about solving problems for users.

UX involves researching user needs, organizing content logically, and designing interactions that create a friendly user experience.

### Design Thinking


```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A[Empathize] --> B[Define]
    B --> C[Ideate]
    C --> D[Prototype]
    D --> E[Test]
    E --> A

    subgraph DesignThinking[Design Thinking Process]
        A
        B
        C
        D
        E
    end

    style A fill:#FFDDC1,stroke:#333,stroke-width:1px
    style B fill:#FFABAB,stroke:#333,stroke-width:1px
    style C fill:#FFC3A0,stroke:#333,stroke-width:1px
    style D fill:#D5AAFF,stroke:#333,stroke-width:1px
    style E fill:#85E3FF,stroke:#333,stroke-width:1px
```

***

![Three lenses of design thinking](https://public-images.interaction-design.org/tags/td-three-lenses-of-design-thinking.png)

*Image by [© Interaction Design Foundation](https://www.interaction-design.org/literature/topics/design-thinking), CC BY-SA 4.0.*

#### Alternative frameworks

- The Double Diamond by the Design Council
- Inspire, Ideate, Implement by IDEO
- Head, Heart and Hand by the American Institution of Graphic Arts (AIGA)

## UI

UI design is about making interfaces beautiful and intuitive.

UI involves choosing colors, typography, layouts, and interactions.

::: {.content-visible unless-format="revealjs"}
## References

- https://www.interaction-design.org/literature/topics/design-thinking
- https://lawsofux.com/
- https://web.stanford.edu/~mshanks/MichaelShanks/files/509554.pdf
:::
